questions: Step 7 (ActionDragState): The current design allows both the library's onActionMoving callback and useClipDrag's pointermove handler to write latestStart/latestEnd. Should the coordinator enforce exclusive ownership (library owns position when crossTrackActive=false, useClipDrag owns when true), or is a simpler 'last writer wins' approach acceptable?

Step 8 (ClipAction stopPropagation): Removing the onPointerDown stopPropagation means the pointerdown event will bubble to the timeline library after useClipDrag's capture-phase handler runs. Have you observed any case where the library's own pointerdown handler causes issues (e.g., starting a competing drag) even when useClipDrag captures the pointer?

Step 9 (height hack): The current hack uses Math.max(200, ...) as a floor. Should the minimum editor pane height be the same MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT (200) already defined in PanesContext, or does the video editor need a different minimum?
success_criteria: useTimelineEditing.ts reduced from ~947 lines to ~250 lines, with 4–5 focused sub-hooks
Zero inline styles on the drop indicator root element (moved to CSS class); dynamic position styles remain inline
Library callback return-value contracts documented with JSDoc on each callback
ActionDragState mutations go through a coordinator API — no direct ref.current[id] = ... outside the coordinator
ClipAction has exactly 2 stopPropagation calls (onClick, onDoubleClick) with explaining comments
EditorPaneTab has no local height subtraction hack — effectiveEditorPaneHeight from PanesContext is used directly
getEffectivePaneHeights handles generations-pane visibility, with updated tests
npm run build passes after every step
Manual smoke test: create/move/resize/drag-drop/delete/split clips all work
assumptions: The timeline library (@xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor) callback return-value semantics (false=reject, undefined=accept) are stable and won't change
The capture-phase pointerdown in useClipDrag (line 425) reliably fires before the library's own drag initialization, so removing ClipAction's onPointerDown stopPropagation won't cause competing drags
MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT (200px) in PanesContext is the correct floor for the video editor as well — no separate minimum needed
The existing CSS classes in timeline-overrides.css (.drop-indicator-row, .drop-indicator-line, etc.) are already styled correctly; only the root container is missing a CSS class
No other consumers of getEffectivePaneHeights exist outside PanesProvider — extending its signature is safe
